input.title: Freelancing Platform
input.description: Freelancing Platform is a web-based enterprise platform that centralizes operational workflows, data visibility, and cross-functional reporting.

user_classes.Admin: Configures the system, manages users, and oversees permissions and settings.
user_classes.Manager: Oversees departmental workflows, approvals, KPIs, and operational reporting.
user_classes.Employee: Performs day-to-day operational tasks within assigned modules and permissions.
user_classes.User: Generic authenticated platform user with access limited by role and department.

definitions.Admin: System user with full administrative privileges.
definitions.Manager: Operational owner of a department, module, or workflow.
definitions.Employee: End user performing role-specific business operations.

constraints: Initial release will focus on the web platform only
Role-based access control is mandatory across all modules
Operational data must be auditable and retained according to policy

operating_environment.frontend: Web-based interface built with Next.js and accessible through modern browsers.
operating_environment.backend: Service layer implemented with Node.js, PostgreSQL, Stripe for business operations.
operating_environment.deployment: Cloud-hosted environment with centralized monitoring, backups, and automated deployments.

NFR-01.title: Security and Access Control
requirements: All sensitive data must be encrypted in transit and at rest
Role-based authorization must restrict access by module and action
All authentication events and privileged actions must be logged

NFR-02.title: Performance and Scalability
requirements: Core pages and dashboards should load within acceptable enterprise SLAs
The platform must support concurrent usage across multiple departments
Background jobs and reporting workloads should not block transactional workflows

NFR-03.title: Reliability and Recoverability
requirements: Automated backups must run on a defined schedule
Critical workflows should support retry-safe processing and audit logging
Operational alerts should be generated for failures and degraded services

system_attributes.reliability: Platform should provide resilient transactional processing with backup and recovery capabilities.
system_attributes.scalability: Architecture should support horizontal scaling for APIs, background workers, and reporting services.
system_attributes.security: Authentication, authorization, encryption, and auditability must be built into every module.
system_attributes.maintainability: Codebase should remain modular, observable, and safe to extend through clear service boundaries.

out_of_scope: Native mobile applications in the first release
Large third-party marketplace or legacy ERP migrations unless explicitly scoped
Non-core experimental modules outside the platform's operational workflows

FR-01.title: Freelancer Registration
requirements: Register with email and create profile
Add skills, hourly rate, and portfolio

FR-03.title: Job Posting
requirements: Post jobs with description, budget, and timeline
Set required skills and experience level
Choose fixed-price or hourly contract type

FR-04.title: Job Search and Proposals
requirements: Search jobs by keyword, skill, and budget
Filter by job type, experience level, and duration
Submit proposals with cover letter and bid

FR-06.title: Escrow Payment System
requirements: Client funds escrow before work starts
Automatic weekly billing for hourly contracts

FR-07.title: Messaging and File Sharing
requirements: Real-time messaging between client and freelancer

FR-09.title: Freelancer Portfolio
requirements: Showcase completed projects with images and descriptions

FR-01.title: Design Freelancer Registration UI
FR-01.description: Design the user interface for freelancer registration. Create the screens, forms, dashboards, or status views needed by the SRS and make the workflow clear, responsive, and accessible for the target users.
acceptance_criteria: UI design for freelancer registration is complete and reviewable.
The freelancer registration design is responsive and accessible.
The design for freelancer registration aligns with the documented user flow and validation states.

FR-01.title: Frontend Implementation of Freelancer Registration
FR-01.description: Implement the frontend behavior for freelancer registration. Build the required user interactions, validation states, loading states, and error handling so the interface supports these behaviors: Register with email and create profile; Add skills, hourly rate, and portfolio; Identity verification process.
acceptance_criteria: Frontend implements the required freelancer registration user interactions.
Validation, loading, and error states for freelancer registration are handled in the UI.
Frontend behavior for freelancer registration is integrated correctly with backend APIs.

FR-01.title: Backend Implementation of Freelancer Registration
FR-01.description: Implement the backend logic for freelancer registration. Build the required APIs, business rules, validation, permissions, persistence, and processing needed to support these behaviors: Register with email and create profile; Add skills, hourly rate, and portfolio; Identity verification process.
acceptance_criteria: Backend logic for freelancer registration is implemented and testable.
APIs and business rules for freelancer registration enforce validation and security requirements.
Data handling for freelancer registration is reliable and supports the required workflow.

FR-01.title: Database Design for Freelancer Registration
FR-01.description: Design the data model and persistence strategy for freelancer registration. Define entities, relationships, indexes, retention rules, and migration changes needed to store and query the workflow reliably.
acceptance_criteria: The data model for freelancer registration supports the required workflow.
Relationships, constraints, and indexes for freelancer registration are defined correctly.
Schema changes for freelancer registration can be applied safely in target environments.

FR-01.title: Security Hardening for Freelancer Registration
FR-01.description: Implement the security controls required for freelancer registration. Cover authentication, authorization, encryption, auditing, abuse prevention, and compliance checks where the SRS or workflow calls for them.
acceptance_criteria: Security controls for freelancer registration enforce the required authentication, authorization, or data-protection rules.
Sensitive operations and failure scenarios for freelancer registration are logged, validated, and protected against misuse.
The implementation of freelancer registration satisfies the relevant privacy, audit, or compliance expectations in the SRS.

FR-01.title: Integrate Freelancer Registration with External Services
FR-01.description: Integrate freelancer registration with the external services or providers referenced by the SRS. Implement secure service connections, payload validation, error handling, and recovery behavior for any third-party dependency involved in the workflow.
acceptance_criteria: External or cross-system integrations required by freelancer registration exchange the expected payloads successfully.
Retry, timeout, and failure handling for freelancer registration integrations are implemented and observable.
Credentials, tokens, and outbound calls used by freelancer registration are configured and secured correctly.

FR-01.title: Testing Freelancer Registration Functionality
FR-01.description: Create and execute tests for freelancer registration. Cover the main success flows, failure scenarios, permission checks, validation rules, integrations, and operational edge cases described in the SRS so the implementation is safe to release.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover the main success and failure scenarios for freelancer registration.
Security, validation, and permission checks for freelancer registration are verified.
Regression coverage exists for the most important freelancer registration behaviors.
